title: Chewy Umami Uruguayan Veal Stew
description: A hearty, slow‑cooked stew featuring tender veal, earthy root vegetables, and a rich umami broth inspired by Uruguayan comfort food.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium‑high heat.
Add the veal cubes and brown them on all sides; remove and set aside.
In the same pot, add onion, carrot, and garlic; cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes to caramelize.
Deglaze with red wine, scraping up browned bits; simmer until reduced, about 3 minutes.
Return the veal to the pot, then add potatoes, beef broth, bouillon cube, bay leaves, oregano, and smoked paprika.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 2 hours until the veal is tender and the broth thickens.
Stir occasionally and add more broth if necessary.
Remove bay leaves, season with salt and pepper to taste.
Let the stew rest for 10 minutes before serving, then garnish with chopped parsley.
